content: We can reframe the intrinsic reward gap between ICM/RND and heuristic curious agents as a function of uncompacted episodic memory noise, and test the hypothesis that fine-tuning a transformer-based episodic memory module to weight stored memories by their intrinsic surprise gradient will eliminate the performance gap between counting-based curious agents and prediction-error methods like ICM/RND in sparse-reward long-horizon tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
